problem text: Consider an at-the-money call option with expiration $T = 1000$ years on a stock $S$ with an initial price of $190$. Determine the value of this option at time $0$ using the Black-Scholes model. Do not use computational tools.
problem solution: Approach this result from an intuitive perspective. If $T = 1000$, the option effectively mirrors the behavior of the underlying asset. This can also be corroborated using the Black-Scholes model, setting $K = S_0$ and $t \rightarrow \infty$.
problem hint: Think about how an option behaves when it is deep-in-the-money. What does it resemble?
